*Name:* Oxwich Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* south wales
*County:* Swansea
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 51.55463, -4.16791
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxwich_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Oxwich%20Castle/@51.554,-4.148,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Oxwich_Castle_4.jpg/330px-Oxwich_Castle_4.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Oxwich Castle is a Grade I listed castle which occupies a position on a wooded headland overlooking Oxwich Bay on the Gower Peninsula, Wales. Although it may occupy the site of an earlier fortification, it is a castle in name only as it is a grand Tudor fortified manor house built in courtyard style.

_Listing 3 of 33_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Laugharne Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* west wales
*County:* Carmarthenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 51.76972222, -4.46194444
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laugharne_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Laugharne%20Castle/@51.7712,-4.4625,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNrU9wWuKAxdoItElJfQWvYC8AORegBSbzij2eqtAlr24za4KNpuQvUZZwFi5JCisjci_31pqefYoneVl2dFBV4d5Dc-il81T-J8u27jtaIs2GGrXcueLg1QWC_QrNom2ALXiQtmcwWpmBRUw=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Laugharne Castle is in Laugharne, Carmarthenshire, Wales. The castle, located on the estuary of the River Tâf, was originally established in 1116. It was rebuilt as a Norman stronghold. There have been many alterations since then, including becoming a Tudor fortified manor house in the sixteenth century. It changed hands twice during the English Civil War, being eventually captured by Parliamentary forces in 1644.

_Listing 5 of 33_

listing.name: Laugharne Castle
listing.desc: Laugharne Castle is in Laugharne, Carmarthenshire, Wales. The castle, located on the estuary of the River Tâf, was originally established in 1116. It was rebuilt as a Norman stronghold. There have been many alterations since then, including becoming a Tudor fortified manor house in the sixteenth century. It changed hands twice during the English Civil War, being eventually captured by Parliamentary forces in 1644.

imageAttribution.description: Dylan admires ....... the medieval Laugharne castle. The foreground shows the rear view of the wooden sculpture of Dylan Thomas set in the Millennium Garden. The engraved detail on the front of the base is shown at the link: 1708269

*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nanteos_Mansion
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Nanteos%20Mansion/@52.3667,-3.8833,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqG1E5eujFOmKXgHO6sH4Lxay5EyrzjFAlnG3N0VamahkDQr4S0So-CQAVyRpE0qO1NUXX1hWvNW82Fwh7VLBoHka17sX8T8yBc=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.nanteos.com/

*Description:*
Nanteos is an 18th-century former country house in Llanbadarn-y-Creuddyn, near Aberystwyth, Ceredigion, Wales. A Grade I listed building, it is now a country house hotel. The gardens and parkland surrounding the mansion are listed on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 8 of 33_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Drimnagh Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* dublin and the pale
*County:* County Dublin
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 53.324973, -6.33284
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drimnagh_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Drimnagh%20Castle/@53.3247,-6.3342,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/2/26/Drymnagh_castle_Dublin_1820.jpg/330px-Drymnagh_castle_Dublin_1820.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.drimnaghcastle.org/

*Description:*
Drimnagh Castle is a Norman castle located in Drimnagh, a suburb of Dublin, Ireland. It is the only remaining castle in Ireland with a flooded moat around it; this moat is fed by a tributary of a local river, the Camac. Drimnagh Castle Secondary School is located next to the castle.

_Listing 9 of 33_

imageAttribution.credit: Excursions through Ireland, Vol. 2. Published: London, Longman

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Howth Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* dublin and the pale
*County:* County Dublin
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 12th-20th century
*Coords:* 53.3862, -6.0794
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howth_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Howth%20Castle/@53.386,-6.068,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZPO-5NLh1gogxoyEkKu28vTh3oBe59xAO4CvBZVxaYB1t7Cwor3v3XcuMjQliNAhlIog37zdLHy_9UejBTyRN__FpLRE3gCKzCo1OHN7crwWcCUTyvwLOGjAqtXJAnJZScTqIVosyjJOqpPCA=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://howthcastle.ie/

*Description:*
Howth Castle is a historic dwelling, originally of Norman origin, that lies by the village of Howth, County Dublin, Ireland; it is sited within a substantial estate. The castle was the ancestral home of the St Lawrence family that had held the area since the Norman Invasion of 1180, and the head of which held the title of Lord Howth, a feudal barony, until circa 1425, Baron Howth to 1767, then Earl of Howth until 1909. The castle and estate were held by distaff heirs, the Gaisford-St Lawrence family, from 1909 to 2019, when they were sold to Tetrarch Capital.

_Listing 10 of 33_

listing.name: Howth Castle
listing.desc: Howth Castle is a historic dwelling, originally of Norman origin, that lies by the village of Howth, County Dublin, Ireland; it is sited within a substantial estate. The castle was the ancestral home of the St Lawrence family that had held the area since the Norman Invasion of 1180, and the head of which held the title of Lord Howth, a feudal barony, until circa 1425, Baron Howth to 1767, then Earl of Howth until 1909. The castle and estate were held by distaff heirs, the Gaisford-St Lawrence family, from 1909 to 2019, when they were sold to Tetrarch Capital.

imageAttribution.description: Howth Castle in Dublin, Ireland viewed from a rocky outcrop on Howth Hill. The ground appears tawnier than usual because of drought.
